Name: Ubuntu
Description: The world's most popular Linux distribution. Backed by Canonical, Ubuntu offers long-term support releases and a huge software ecosystem. Perfect for newcomers and professionals alike.

Name: Arch Linux
Description: A minimalist, rolling-release distro for advanced users. Install only what you need. The Arch Wiki is the most comprehensive Linux documentation available anywhere.

Name: Fedora
Description: A cutting-edge, community-driven distro sponsored by Red Hat. Ships the latest GNOME and kernel. The proving ground for RHEL technologies.

Name: Linux Mint
Description: The most Windows-friendly Linux distribution. Cinnamon desktop, familiar UI, excellent hardware support. Highly recommended for users switching from Windows.

Name: NixOS
Description: A unique distro where the entire system is declared in a single configuration file. Reproducible, atomic upgrades, rollback support. The future of Linux system management.

Name: Tails
Description: A live OS focused entirely on privacy. Routes all traffic through Tor. Leaves no trace on the host computer. Trusted by journalists, activists, and privacy advocates worldwide.

Name: openSUSE Tumbleweed
Description: A rock-solid rolling-release distro from SUSE. YaST system management, excellent KDE integration, and the most powerful installer in the Linux ecosystem.

Name: Void Linux
Description: An independent distro that does things differently: runit init, XBPS package manager, musl libc option. Lightweight, fast, and uncompromisingly minimal.
